studyName: Genome Wide Association Study of Chronic TMD: Discovery Phase

tables.description: The dataset provides information obtained through application of the following questionnaires: APM (Anthropomorphic Measurements), CPSQ (Comprehensive Pain and Symptom Questionnaire) , EPQ (Eysenck Personality Questionnaire-Revised) , gynecological questionnaire (including hormone directed events/contraceptives/hormone replacement/medications, etc.), IVC (In Vivo Coping), JFLS (Jaw Functional Limitation Scale), Kohn (Kohn Reactivity Scale), LES (Life Experiences Survey), LSL (Lifetime Stressor List), Medical History, OBC (Oral Behaviors Checklist), OPPERA (sociodemograhic and administrative), PCS (Pain Catastrophizing Scale), PILL (Pennebaker Inventory of Limbic Languidness), POMS (Profile of Mood States-Bipolar), PSQI (Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index), PSS (Perceived Stress Scale), QST (Quantitative Sensory Testing (Mechanical/Temperature), RDC (Research Diagnostic Criteria - description of pain location/effect), SCL90 (Symptom Check List 90), SF12 (physical/psychological health/pain interference), SPSR (B/F - Screening Pain Self Report), STAI-Y1 (State-Trait Anxiety Inventory Y1), STAI-Y2 (State-Trait Anxiety Inventory Y2).

variables.name: CPSQ_SIMPLIFIED.ABDOMEN_PAIN_3M
variables.description: CPSQ 51. In the past 3 months, did you have discomfort or pain in your abdomen that was present during at least 3 weeks (lasting at least one day each week)? (For women do not consider abdominal discomfort or pain related to your menstrual cycle or period.)

variables.name: CPSQ_SIMPLIFIED.ABDOMEN_PAIN_BM3_3M

tables.description: The dataset provides information about pain (thermal/pressure) threshold, TMFJ case/control classification, existence of jaw injuries,  and summary scores obtained through: PILL (Pennebaker Inventory of Limbic Languidness), PSS (Perceived Stress Scale), and negative impact items of LES  (Life Experiences Survey), in addition to basic sociodemographic and administrative data.
